comment: Track H.1 PII registry — every Prisma model carrying personal data MUST declare its erasure treatment here (checked by scripts/check-pii-registry.mjs in CI). Treatments: 'erase' = hard-delete the row on a GDPR Art.17 request; 'anonymize' = blank/scramble PII fields but keep the row (referenced by legal business documents or audit trails); 'retain-legal-hold' = statutory retention overrides erasure until the hold period lapses, then anonymize. Erasure runs must produce an auditable report (gdpr.service.ts — runtime half of H.1).

User.rationale: Referenced as author/actor across audit and business trails; scramble identity fields, keep the id.
User.reviewed: 2026-07-18 initial classification (cycle 12) — pending human compliance review

Organization.rationale: Tenant organization contact data; anonymize contact fields on offboarding (H.2 lifecycle owns full purge).

Employee.rationale: Labor/tax law mandates multi-year retention of employment records; anonymize after the statutory period.

Customer.rationale: Counterparty on legally retained documents (invoices, orders); PII fields anonymized, document rows kept.

Vendor.rationale: Counterparty on legally retained procurement/AP documents.

Contact.rationale: CRM person record with no statutory retention of its own.

Lead.rationale: Prospect data; no legal basis to retain after erasure request.

POSLoyaltyMember.rationale: Loyalty membership PII; erase on request, points ledger anonymized via member-id unlink.

Applicant.rationale: Recruitment data; short-lived by design (also subject to jurisdictional application-data limits).

CustomerPortalUser.rationale: Portal login identity; erase with the customer-side request.

VendorPortalUser.rationale: Portal login identity; erase with the vendor-side request.
